The core insight: {{CORE_INSIGHT}}

What {{PRODUCT_NAME}} Does

For {{USER_TYPE_1}}

  1. {{STEP_1}}
  2. {{STEP_2}}
  3. {{STEP_3}}

Build Options

Two ways to move forward — choose what works best for {{CLIENT_SHORT}} right now.

Recommended

Partnership Model

No upfront build cost

$0 upfront

${{MONTHLY_FEE}}/mo + {{REV_SHARE_PCT}}% per completed job

  • RubyxQube builds the app at no charge
  • RubyxQube maintains it — {{CLIENT_SHORT}} never touches code
  • App updates, bug fixes, and new features all included
  • {{PARTNER_ACCESS_NOTE}}
  • ${{MONTHLY_FEE}}/mo covers hosting, database, and auth infrastructure
  • RubyxQube receives {{REV_SHARE_PCT}}% of each completed job value

The platform fee covers real infrastructure costs. RubyxQube's profit comes from the {{REV_SHARE_PCT}}% — we only earn when you earn.

One-Time Build

You own it outright

${{FLAT_FEE}} flat fee
  • Full app built and delivered to {{CLIENT_SHORT}}
  • All revenue kept 100% by {{CLIENT_SHORT}}
  • No per-job fee to RubyxQube
  • Ongoing maintenance billed separately at ${{MAINTENANCE_FEE}}/mo
  • Code changes and new features billed hourly

Without ongoing maintenance the app will fall behind platform updates over time.

Why the Partnership Model makes more sense: {{PARTNERSHIP_RATIONALE}}

How the Revenue Share Works

RubyxQube receives {{REV_SHARE_PCT}}% of the actual completed job value — paid by {{CLIENT_SHORT}} only after a job is confirmed complete.

{{CLIENT_SHORT}} keeps
{{CLIENT_SHARE_PCT}}%
of every completed job value
RubyxQube receives
{{REV_SHARE_PCT}}%
of every completed job value

How it scales across different job sizes:

Job Type Job Value {{CLIENT_SHORT}} ({{CLIENT_SHARE_PCT}}%) RubyxQube ({{REV_SHARE_PCT}}%)
{{JOB_TYPE_1}}${{VALUE_1}}${{CLIENT_CUT_1}}${{RXQ_CUT_1}}
{{JOB_TYPE_2}}${{VALUE_2}}${{CLIENT_CUT_2}}${{RXQ_CUT_2}}
{{JOB_TYPE_3}}${{VALUE_3}}${{CLIENT_CUT_3}}${{RXQ_CUT_3}}

The {{REV_SHARE_PCT}}% fee applies only to jobs confirmed completed through the platform. Quotes, estimates, and leads that don't convert are not charged.

Build Timeline

{{TIMELINE_INTRO}}

Phase 1Weeks 1–2
{{PHASE_1_TITLE}} Up Next

{{PHASE_1_DESC}}
Phase 2Weeks 3–4
{{PHASE_2_TITLE}} — {{PHASE_2_DESC}}
Phase 3TBD
{{PHASE_3_TITLE}} — {{PHASE_3_DESC}}

Target Phase 1 launch: {{LAUNCH_TARGET}}

Existing Website — Momentum Plan

Separate from the app, {{CLIENT_NAME}} is enrolled on RubyxQube's Momentum Plan — the full-service monthly growth package covering the marketing website, AI receptionist, and ongoing local SEO.

Momentum Plan — What's Included

  • Full custom website — built, hosted, and maintained
  • Custom AI receptionist trained on {{CLIENT_SHORT}}'s services, pricing, and FAQs
  • 24/7 lead capture — answers questions, qualifies prospects around the clock
  • Instant alert when a new lead comes in
  • Google Business Profile management
  • Local SEO monitoring & updates
  • Priority same-day edits
  • Review response management
  • Monthly 20-minute strategy call
  • Monthly report: visits, chats, leads captured
PlanSetup FeeMonthly Rate
Momentum {{MOMENTUM_SETUP}} $699/mo

The Momentum Plan is an independent ongoing engagement. The {{REV_SHARE_PCT}}% per-job fee does not offset or replace the Momentum Plan retainer.

Next Steps

To move forward, the following decisions are needed:

  • {{NEXT_STEP_1}}
  • {{NEXT_STEP_2}}
  • {{NEXT_STEP_3}}
  • {{NEXT_STEP_4}}
  • Sign a simple partnership agreement outlining platform ownership, per-job fee, and exit terms.

Why This Works for Both Parties

{{CLIENT_NAME}} brings {{CLIENT_BRINGS}}. RubyxQube brings the technology platform and ongoing maintenance. Neither party can fully build this alone.

{{CLIENT_SHORT}} does not need to invest capital upfront. A ${{MONTHLY_FEE}}/mo platform fee covers infrastructure, and RubyxQube's {{REV_SHARE_PCT}}% only kicks in when a job is completed. As {{CLIENT_SHORT}} grows its partner network, RubyxQube grows with it — our incentives are perfectly aligned.

This proposal is a draft for discussion. All figures are negotiable and subject to a formal agreement. Prepared by Boyd Querubin, RubyxQube — boyd@rubyxqube.com
